name: Let's Encrypt
description: Lets Encrypt — free, automated, and open certificate authority by the Internet Security Research Group.

seo.title: Let's Encrypt — Secrets Management
seo.meta_description: Let's Encrypt: Lets Encrypt — free, automated, and open certificate authority by the Internet Security Research Group.

when_to_use: Free ACME TLS certificate issuance for public web services
Wildcard certificate via DNS-01 challenge for subdomains
Auto-renewal via certbot or acme.sh for zero-downtime certs
HTTPS enablement for open-source and nonprofit projects
CI/CD cert provisioning for ephemeral staging environments

pros: Free, automated, universally trusted DV certs
Huge community, countless tutorials and clients
cons: DV only; no EV or OV available
Rate limits can bite at scale
No commercial SLA or support contract
review.verdict: The default choice for public TLS in 2026; almost no reason not to use it for standard websites.
review.best_for: Any site needing free, automated public TLS with broad client support.
review.not_for: Cases legally requiring EV, OV, or paid CA contracts.

pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Free / hobby tier; no peak-lock evidence.
